title: Stend IV
content:

'''Stend IV''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that lay in the [[Juris sector/Legends|Juris sector]]. Located on the [[Five Veils Route]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], it was a [[Temperature/Legends|tropical]] world with both [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]] and [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] on the surface. The [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] inhabitants of the planet [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]] [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] Stend IV [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] in an attempt to locate new sources of energy.

Shortly after the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], Stend IV, which was also [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to [[Elomin/Legends|Elomin]] colonists, was targeted by the [[Terrorist/Legends|eco-terrorist]] group known as the [[Army of Life]], which conspired with the [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentrist]] group called the [[Knights of the Core]]. Their plan was to [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggle]] [[Egg/Legends|eggs]] of [[clodhopper]]s, [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]] [[Species/Legends|creatures]] native to Naboo and infamous for causing the devastation of whole planetary ecosystems, to Stend IV.

The [[Army/Legends|Army]] of Life hired a group of individuals that [[Transport/Legends|transported]] the clodhopper eggs to a [[Gev-Toosa|settlement]] on the Gran colony world. Despite an [[Showdown on Stend IV|attack]] by a [[Dree-Tekes|Jedi Guardian]]; a few Gran [[Militia/Legends|militiamen]]; and another, [[Politics/Legends|government]]-recruited group of individuals, the terrorist affiliates succeeded in releasing the clodhoppers into the environment of Stend IV.

==Description==
Stend IV was a remote [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" /> located within the [[Stend system/Legends|Stend system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Juris sector/Legends|Juris sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The planet occupied the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position around its [[Star/Legends|star]]<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" /> and lay on the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]]<ref name="EAOC" /> known as the [[Five Veils Route]],<ref name="The Essential Atlas Stend system" /> which connected it to the [[Ninzam system/Legends|Ninzam]] and [[Lybeya system/Legends|Vergesso systems]].<ref name="EAOC" />

Stend IV had at least one continent on its surface,<ref name="Secrets of Naboo" /> as well as several clear, [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]]. Land was dominated by lush, green [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]]. The climate was [[Temperature/Legends|tropical]], with white [[Cloud/Legends|clouds]] in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] obscuring much of the planet's surface when viewed from [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. The exact composition of Stend IV's atmosphere was different enough from the air on Naboo or aboard the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Majestic Gundark]]'' to render the use of chemically-bonded locks practical.<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" />

==History==
[[File:GranNEGAS.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Stend IV was a colony world of the Gran.]]
[[Standard decade/Legends|Decades]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Stend IV was settled by [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] from the fellow colony world of [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]]. The settlement was an attempt by Malastare to find new sources of energy for exploitation; that goal had not been reached, however, by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[The galaxy/Legends|Galactic]] Civil War. The Gran colonies, which also included [[Hok/Legends|Hok]], [[Koensayr]], and [[Silunes]], were at first considered to be prestigious by the wider [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], but they eventually fell out of favor with the Gran [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kinyen/Legends|Kinyen]] due to rampant vice and violence on the colony worlds.<ref name="Strongholds of Resistance">''[[Strongholds of Resistance]]''</ref>

Shortly after the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|ended]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="Operation Clodhopper timeframe">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20010222part1|text=Operation: Clodhopper Part I: Rendezvous at Frundle's|int=Operation: Clodhopper}} is set "just after the events of Episode I," which refers to [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''The Phantom Menace'', which includes the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> the [[Terrorist/Legends|eco-terrorist]] group known as the [[Army of Life]] formulated a plan to attack the settlers of Stend IV for [[Politics/Legends|political]] gain. To that end, the [[Army/Legends|Army]] of Life, which had anti-technology goals and preached acts of violence against [[City/Legends|settlements]] on various worlds, conspired with<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" /> the [[Knights of the Core]], a [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] terrorist group that wanted to target a continent on Stend IV inhabited by Gran and [[Elomin/Legends|Elomin]] colonists.<ref name="Secrets of Naboo" />

The Army of Life took advantage of the recently established peace between Naboo's [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Human]] and [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] populations to [[Thief/Legends|steal]] 400 [[clodhopper]] [[Egg/Legends|eggs]]. An [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]] [[Species/Legends|pest]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to Naboo and infamous for destroying whole planetary ecosystems due to a voracious appetite and rapid rate of [[Reproduction/Legends|reproduction]], clodhoppers were even known to [[Death/Legends|consume]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] inhabitants of the worlds they infested.<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" />

{{RPG|"[[Operation: Clodhopper]]"}}
The Army of Life subsequently hired a group of individuals to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''Majestic Gundark'' with two [[Cargo container/Legends|cargo containers]] aboard from Naboo to Stend IV. The group was to deliver the "bio-material"—which, unbeknownst to the group, was the clodhopper eggs—inside the containers to the largest settlement on Stend IV and release it into the environment. The freighter successfully traveled to Stend IV,<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" /> albeit pursued by a government-tasked group of individuals on a mission to stop the terrorist plot.<ref name="Secrets of Naboo" />

[[File:Clodhopper.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Stend IV was threatened by the introduction of clodhoppers.]]
By that time, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" /><ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> had also learned of the theft of the clodhoppers and thus dispatched the relatively inexperienced Gran [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Jedi Guardian]] [[Dree-Tekes]] to Stend IV in order to prevent the terrorist plan from succeeding. Just as the freighter carrying the clodhoppers landed outside the settlement of [[Gev-Toosa]], Dree-Tekes led a small group of local Gran settlers she had armed in an [[Showdown on Stend IV|attack]] on the [[Transport/Legends|transport]].<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" /> Although the government-recruited group also attacked the terrorist affiliates,<ref name="Secrets of Naboo" /> the latter prevailed in the intense skirmish and successfully released the clodhopper eggs into the environment.<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
By the time the Invasion of Naboo was repelled,<ref name="Operation Clodhopper timeframe" /> Gran settlers had colonized Stend IV. The colony world was at a low level of technology and did not maintain a [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary defensive shield]]. The only military presence on the planet was a rag-tag colonial [[Militia/Legends|militia]], with only four Gran members actually having military experience, albeit limited.<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" /> One continent on Stend IV's surface was also home to Elomin colonists.<ref name="Secrets of Naboo" />

{{RPG|"[[Operation: Clodhopper]]"}}
During the clodhopper threat on Stend IV, the Gran [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Dree-Tekes gave the four Gran militiamen who had prior military experience primitive weapons, including [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]], an antiquated [[Blaster cannon/Legends|heavy blaster cannon]], and a [[Grenade launcher/Legends|grenade launcher]]. The militiamen fought zealously under the leadership of the Jedi to prevent the endangerment of their world, but in the end their adversaries were successful in releasing the clodhoppers into the planet's environment.<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" />
{{Endgame}}

==Locations==
There were only a few isolated settlements on Stend IV, of which the only one large enough to be considered a town was Gev-Toosa. Consisting of artificially constructed structures of refined metals, Gev-Toosa served as the planet's [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. It did not have a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], instead relying on an automated signal that instructed new arrivals to land outside Gev-Toosa and walk to the settlement.<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SecretsofNaboo.jpg|thumb|right|180px|''Secrets of Naboo'', which introduced Stend IV]]
Stend IV was first mentioned in ''[[Secrets of Naboo]]'', a ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook written by [[Steve Miller]] and [[J.D. Wiker]], and published by [[Wizards of the Coast]]<ref name="Secrets of Naboo" /> on [[February 8]], [[2001]].<ref name="Secrets of Naboo date">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010131.html|text=Naboo's Secrets Revealed}}</ref> The book mentioned the planet in an adventure seed named "The Defoliation Agenda."<ref name="Secrets of Naboo" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Stend [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Stend IV, in grid square P-17.<ref name="The Essential Atlas" />

In "The Defoliation Agenda," which, like the rest of ''Secrets of Naboo'', is set during the Trade Federation [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] of Naboo, the player characters are called upon by a government official to stop a plan by the Knights of the Core terrorist group to release a cargo infested with clodhoppers on Stend IV. The characters confront the terrorists at a Naboo [[Kwilaan Starport/Legends|spaceport]] and pursue their freighter to Stend IV, where they have to convince the Elomin officials of the local starport of the impending threat. After being given permission to search the starport and impound the infested cargo containers, the characters face the terrorists and have to defeat them, as well as track down and stop a lone [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] that manages to leave the port with the dangerous cargo.<ref name="Secrets of Naboo" />

The adventure seed also presented an alternate plot line in which the player characters were, in fact, the group of individuals affiliated with the Knights of the Core. In this variation, the characters have to smuggle the clodhoppers off Naboo and onto Stend IV, thwarting the efforts of opposing parties on both planets.<ref name="Secrets of Naboo" />

The first proper appearance of Stend IV was in "[[Operation: Clodhopper]]," a ''Secrets of Naboo'' tie-in mini-adventure published on [[Wizards.com]] [[February 22|two weeks]] after the sourcebook. "Operation: Clodhopper" was an expanded and slightly modified version of the alternate variant of "The Defoliation Agenda." This article assumes that as many events as possible of both "Operation: Clodhopper" and the original version of the ''Secrets of Naboo'' adventure seed play out as described, merging the plot lines of the two scenarios. Wherever contradictions arise, the online mini-adventure is assumed to take precedence due to its status as a more recent and fleshed-out source.<ref name="Operation Clodhopper" />
